name: Chocolate Cinnamon Rolls
recipeIngredient: 1/2 cup Milk, lukewarm (scald, then cool to lukewarm)
recipeInstructions:
------------------POWDERED SUGAR FROSTING------------------
1 cup Powdered sugar, sifted
1 tablespoon Milk
1/2 teaspoon Vanilla
1. Dissolve the yeast in the warm water in a large bowl.
2. Stir in the milk, 1/4 cup of the sugar, the shortening, egg, and salt.
3. Mix the cocoa with the one and one quarter cups of flour and add to the yeast mixture.
4. Mix with a spoon until smooth. Add enough of the remaining flour to make the dough easy to handle, but still slightly sticky and soft.
5. Turn out on a lightly floured board and knead until smooth and elastic, about 8-10 minutes.
6. Let the dough rest while you wash the mixing bowl, dry it, and grease it with shortening.
7. Put the dough into the bowl, turning to grease the top.
8. Cover and refrigerate for 3-4 days, punching down when too large for the bowl.
9. Cover and let rise in a warm place until double, about 1-1/2 hours (dough is ready when an indentation remains when touched).
10. Punch the dough down and roll into a rectangle 12 x 9 inches.
11. Spread the rectangle with softened margarine.
12. Mix the 1/4 cup of sugar and cinnamon together and sprinkle over the buttered dough.
13. Roll up beginning at the 12-inch side; when fully rolled, pinch the seam to seal.
14. Cut the roll into 12 slices and place slightly apart in a baking pan (9 x 9 x 2 inches). For larger rolls, use a 13 x 9 x 2 inch pan and let rise until it fills the pan.
15. Cover and let rise for about 40 minutes.
16. Preheat oven to 375°F.
17. Bake for 25-30 minutes.
18. Spread with powdered sugar frosting while still warm. Mix frosting ingredients together until reaching a spreading consistency.
19. Makes enough frosting for 12 rolls. Increase amounts for larger rolls.
